title: Balanced Parentheses Checker: Miscounted Closing Bracket

statement.markdown: Given an array of characters representing parentheses, brackets, and curly braces, determine if the elements are balanced. A string is balanced if every opening bracket has a corresponding matching closing bracket. However, the given implementation miscounts the closing bracket counts. Fix the bug to solve the task correctly.
statement.inputFormat: An array of characters containing parentheses, brackets, and curly braces.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the string is balanced.
statement.notes: Note that a string with an unbalanced opening bracket but a matching closing bracket is not balanced.

hints.text: Use a stack data structure to track opening brackets.
Compare the stack elements with the current closing bracket.

editorial.title: Balanced Parentheses Checker
editorial.intuition: A stack-based approach can efficiently track opening brackets and their corresponding closing brackets.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Check each character from left to right, updating the counts of opening and closing brackets manually.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: Create a dictionary mapping opening brackets to their corresponding closing brackets and maintain a stack of opening brackets for efficient matching.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Prove that if the stack is empty at the end of the process and the counts of opening and closing brackets are equal, the string is balanced.
editorial.dryRun: Example run of the optimal solution.
